title: Blazer Architecture Fingerprint
description: A layered fingerprint describing the architecture of a software application. Layer 1 captures code-identity facts (purl/CPE/language). Layer 2 captures environment facts as typed facets drawn from controlled vocabularies (CNCF Landscape IDs, OTEL semantic conventions, cloud service namespaces, purl). Layer 3 is a set of matched archetypes derived from Layers 1 and 2.

source.description:
Hashed identifiers for the source repository. Plain URLs, commit SHAs, and branch names are NEVER stored — the detector hashes them locally before transmission so Blazer can recognize the same repo across submissions without learning its location or identity.

CONSTRUCTION. All *_hash fields are HMAC-SHA-256(tenant_hash_key, canonical_value), hex-encoded. The `tenant_hash_key` is a 256-bit random value minted by Blazer and scoped to a tenant. The detector fetches it during API-key exchange and caches it locally; all users of the tenant receive the same key, so two users fingerprinting the same repo produce the same hashes. Rotating the key breaks correlation with prior submissions — an acceptable cost when the key is believed leaked.

THREAT MODEL. Blazer holds both the stored hashes and the tenant key, so Blazer is technically capable of brute-forcing a hash back to a candidate URL (the public-repo URL space is small). This is considered acceptable because (a) Blazer does not do so as a matter of policy, and (b) the attacker who would benefit most from deanonymization — Blazer itself — already sees the submitting tenant's identity by virtue of the API key. A leaked tenant key would let an external attacker perform the same brute force against copies of the hashes they obtained; compared to storing raw URLs, the leak surface is strictly smaller and the compromise is tenant-scoped.

See docs/fingerprint/README for the detector-side reference implementation.

hash_algorithm.description: Algorithm used for all *_hash fields. Fixed at hmac-sha256; versioned so it can be rotated later without ambiguity.

repo_hash.description:
HMAC-SHA-256(tenant_hash_key, canonical_repo_url), hex-encoded.

Canonicalization before hashing: (1) if the input is SSH-form (`git@host:org/repo.git`), rewrite to `https://host/org/repo`; (2) strip any userinfo (`user:pass@`); (3) strip a trailing `.git`; (4) strip a trailing `/`; (5) normalize the scheme to `https`; (6) lowercase the entire URL (most forges treat org/repo paths case-insensitively, so lowercasing ensures two users who type the same repo with different casing produce the same hash). The result is hashed as UTF-8 bytes.

commit_hash.description: HMAC-SHA-256(tenant_hash_key, full 40-char lowercase commit SHA), hex-encoded. Allows correlating a fingerprint to a specific commit across submissions without revealing the SHA itself.

branch_hash.description: Optional HMAC-SHA-256(tenant_hash_key, branch_name). Useful for distinguishing long-lived feature branches that may have materially different architectures. Omit entirely for extra anonymity on side branches — the absence of the field is itself not a tell, since the default branch is the common case.

key_version.description: Version of the tenant_hash_key used to produce the hashes in this submission. Bumped by Blazer on key rotation; allows the backend to distinguish 'same repo under old key' from 'different repo' when correlating across a rotation event.

detector.description: Tool or agent that produced the fingerprint (e.g., 'blazer-claude-plugin').

languages.description: Layer 1 — source languages detected, using GitHub Linguist IDs (lowercased).

packages.description: Layer 1 — package dependencies identified by Package URL (purl). CycloneDX-compatible.

cpe.description: Optional CPE 2.3 URI for commercial / OS-level products.

direct.description: True if declared directly in a manifest (vs. transitive).

manifest.description: Repo-relative path to the manifest that declared this dep.

facets.description: Layer 2 — environment facts. Each facet is a multi-valued set of canonical IDs drawn from a controlled vocabulary. A facet may be empty or omitted when unknown. Ambiguity is represented by multiple values with confidence scores.

runtime.description: Execution runtime (e.g., otel:nodejs, otel:python, otel:ruby, otel:jvm).

framework.description: Primary application framework (purl or CNCF ID).

package_manager.description: e.g., cncf:npm, cncf:pnpm, cncf:bundler, cncf:pip, cncf:poetry.

ci_cd.description: CI/CD platform (CNCF Landscape IDs: github-actions, circleci, buildkite, gitlab-ci, ...).

iac.description: Infrastructure as Code (cncf:terraform, cncf:pulumi, aws:cdk, aws:sam, cncf:crossplane).

container_build.description: Image build tooling (cncf:docker, cncf:buildkit, cncf:buildpacks, cncf:ko, cncf:jib).

orchestration.description: Runtime orchestrator (cncf:kubernetes, cloud:aws/ecs, cloud:aws/eks, cloud:gcp/gke, cncf:nomad).

cloud_provider.description: OTEL cloud.provider values: otel:aws, otel:gcp, otel:azure, otel:cloudflare, otel:heroku, ...

cloud_platform.description: Server-side runtime platform. OTEL cloud.platform values: otel:aws_lambda, otel:aws_ecs, otel:gcp_cloud_run, otel:azure_container_apps, ...

client_platform.description: Target OS/runtime for the compiled artifact, when the app is a client (mobile, desktop, browser). Values: otel:ios, otel:android, otel:ipados, otel:watchos, otel:tvos, otel:macos, otel:windows, otel:linux, otel:web. Multi-valued for apps that target more than one (e.g., a KMP app targets android + ios).

build_system.description: Local build toolchain for client/mobile/desktop artifacts — distinct from container_build (OCI images). Values: tool:xcode, tool:xcodebuild, cncf:gradle, cncf:bazel, tool:msbuild, tool:cmake, tool:cargo, tool:swift-build.

distribution.description: Distribution / release channel for the compiled artifact. Values: saas:app-store, saas:testflight, saas:play-store, saas:firebase-app-distribution, saas:microsoft-store, saas:mac-app-store, saas:homebrew-cask, cncf:fdroid, saas:direct-download, saas:enterprise-mdm.

datastore.description: Primary OLTP/data stores (cloud:aws/rds-postgres, cloud:aws/dynamodb, pkg:generic/postgres, pkg:generic/mysql, ...).

messaging.description: Queues / streams (cloud:aws/sqs, cloud:aws/sns, cloud:aws/eventbridge, cncf:kafka, cncf:nats).

evidence.description: Raw signals that justified facet/archetype inferences. Indices are referenced by evidence_refs on facet values and archetypes.

matched.description: The concrete value or pattern that matched.

supports.description: Facet keys or archetype IDs this evidence supports.

archetypes.description: Layer 3 — archetype IDs matched, derived from facets + packages via archetype predicates.

id.description: Archetype ID matching an entry in archetypes.yaml.

matched_predicates.description: List of facet keys (or 'requires'/'any_of' group labels) that matched.

notes.description: Free-form notes from the detector (should not be relied on for matching).

confidence.description: 0 = pure guess, 1 = observed directly in source.

id.description: Canonical identifier prefixed by vocabulary: 'cncf:<item-id>', 'otel:<value>', 'cloud:<provider>/<service>', 'purl:<purl>', 'saas:<vendor>', 'tool:<tool>' (for proprietary developer tooling not in CNCF/OTEL, e.g., tool:xcode, tool:msbuild).
